target.title: Support dragging Codex account tabs
target.body:
## Summary
- Add drag-to-reorder behavior to the Codex multi-account segmented switcher in the menu.
- Persist the reordered visible Codex account IDs in user defaults and apply that order to the visible account projection.
- Add focused tests for switcher reordering, defaults persistence, and projection ordering.

## Notes
- Clicking still selects an account; dragging past the movement threshold reorders instead of selecting.
- Dragging now previews the reorder with a floating account tab plus an in-row placeholder slot, so neighboring tabs slide into place before drop.
- Reordered tabs rebuild the open menu so the persisted order is reflected by subsequent menu renders.

## Privacy-safe visual proof
I avoided attaching a real desktop recording because the Codex menu can expose private account labels, local usage state, and surrounding desktop context.

Instead, this proof uses synthetic account labels and a simplified Codex menu mock:
- Dragging a Codex account tab creates a floating tab and placeholder/drop slot.
- Neighboring tabs slide into the preview order.
- Reopening the menu restores the reordered account order from the local switcher preference.

No real account labels, desktop content, provider data, tokens, or user-specific state are included.

![Synthetic drag reorder proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iambinlin/CodexBar/proof/codexbar-drag-reorder-assets/docs/proofs/codexbar-drag-reorder-proof.gif)

![Privacy-safe PRD proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iambinlin/CodexBar/proof/codexbar-drag-reorder-assets/docs/proofs/codexbar-drag-reorder-prd.png)

## Validation
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `./Scripts/lint.sh format` passed: 0/985 files formatted.
- `swift build --target CodexBar` passed after a temporary local SwiftUI `@Entry` compatibility replacement because the installed Command Line Tools lack `SwiftUIMacros`; source was restored afterward.
- `./.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` passed: 0/985 files require formatting.
- `swift test --filter CodexAccountSwitcherDragTests` could not complete on this machine because the local Command Line Tools build fails dependency compilation at `KeyboardShortcuts` `#Preview` macros (`PreviewsMacros` plugin not found).
- `make check` could not complete because SwiftLint/SourceKitten fails to load `sourcekitdInProc.framework` from the installed Command Line Tools; SwiftFormat and parser hash checks completed before that failure.
target.author: iambinlin
